Motion To Compel Defendant Salma & Companys Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LAURIE BURKE's Motion To Compel Defendant Salma & Company's Further Responses To Discovery.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is DENIED. On February 8, 2022, this Court signed an order denying Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to the same discovery propounded against Salma & Company. JPT Paul Renne found that the Plaintiff failed to file and serve separate statements for the interrogatories and document requests at issue. A notice of motion to compel further responses must be served within 45 days after verified responses, or any verified supplemental responses, were served unless the parties agree in writing to extend the time. CCP Sec. 2030.300(c). Although the Motion was denied without prejudice, the present motion was not filed within 45-days of service of the verified responses. The Court lacks jurisdiction to order further answers after 45 days. See Vidal Sassoon, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Halpern) (1983) 147 CA3d 681, 685. All requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.
